sed -n '170,200p' .sdlc/tools/dev-driver/tool.ts

# The issue is that `node ${toolPath}` doesn't work for TypeScript files
# It needs tsx/ts-node. Let's try running the quality-check with node via tsx
echo '{}' | npx --yes tsx .sdlc/tools/quality-check/tool.ts --run 2>&1

# The quality-check isn't reading the config - let's debug it
# Read the parseChecks function
sed -n '115,175p' .sdlc/tools/quality-check/tool.ts

thinking.thinking: The issue is the quality-check config uses `script:` not `command:`. Let me fix the config to use `script:` instead of `command:`.

input.command:
# The config needs `script:` not `command:` - fix it
cat > .sdlc/tools/quality-check/config.yaml << 'EOF'
# quality-check tool configuration
name: quality-check
version: "0.1.0"

checks:
  - name: "failing-test-check"
    description: "Simulated failing check for UAT testing"
    script: "exit 1"
EOF

echo "Config written, running quality-check..."
echo '{}' | bun .sdlc/tools/quality-check/tool.ts --run 2>&1

# Quality check now fails. Now test dev-driver blocking
echo '{}' | bun .sdlc/tools/dev-driver/tool.ts --run 2>/tmp/dev-driver-quality-stderr2.txt | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null
echo "---STDERR---"
cat /tmp/dev-driver-quality-stderr2.txt | head -10
input.description: Test 3: dev-driver blocked by failing quality check

thinking.thinking:
I see the problem now. The dev-driver uses:
```
const raw = execSync(`node ${toolPath} --run`, { ... })
```

`node` can't run TypeScript files directly, so the quality-check tool fails (because it throws an error trying to run a TS file), but dev-driver catches the error and treats it as "no failures" (line 195: `log.warn(\`quality-check execution error: ${e} — treating as no failures\`)`).

This is a BUG in dev-driver: it uses `node` to run the quality-check, which fails because TS files can't be run directly by `node`. 

The quality-check tool needs to be invoked with `bun` or `npx tsx` instead. Looking at how the server handles this in tool_runner.rs, it properly uses bun/deno/node+tsx depending on the file type, but dev-driver is bypassing that and calling `node` directly on the TypeScript file. That's why Test 3 is failing — the quality check blocking mechanism never actually runs because the invocation fails silently and gets treated as a success.

Wait, but if all features are marked as released, was this bug shipped? Let me trace through the dev-driver code more carefully to see if it's using the same detection logic as tool_runner.rs. Looking at the actual invocation, it's hardcoded to use `node ${toolPath}` without any TypeScript runtime detection, so it will always fail when quality-check has issues to report. The stderr output shows the quality-check process is starting but the error gets swallowed somewhere in the execution chain.

Actually, I see what's happening now—quality-check writes JSON to stdout and exits with code 1 when there are failures, but `execSync` throws an error on non-zero exit codes. The dev-driver catches that error and treats it as "no failures found," which means the quality gate gets completely bypassed. This is definitely a bug—the dev-driver needs to either handle the exit code differently or parse the JSON output regardless of whether the process exits successfully. The real issue is that `execSync` throws on non-zero exit codes, but dev-driver is catching that exception and incorrectly treating it as "no failures" — when actually quality-check ran successfully and found real failures. The fix is to either suppress the throw behavior with proper options or switch to `spawnSync` to handle the exit code explicitly.

Now I need to document this bug and move on to testing the other cases, starting by restoring the original config.

input.command:
# Restore original quality-check config
cp /tmp/quality-check-config-backup.yaml .sdlc/tools/quality-check/config.yaml
rm -f .sdlc/.dev-driver.lock
echo "Restored config and removed lock"

# The Test 3 bug: execSync throws on non-zero exit but dev-driver treats it as "no failures"
# Let's verify this is indeed the bug
grep -n "execSync\|spawnSync\|catch\|treating as no failures" .sdlc/tools/dev-driver/tool.ts | head -15
input.description: Document Test 3 bug and restore config

input.command: sed -n '178,200p' .sdlc/tools/dev-driver/tool.ts
input.description: Read quality-check invocation code for bug analysis

thinking.thinking:
I've confirmed the bug:1. `execSync(\`node ${toolPath} --run\`, ...)` - uses `node` to run the TS file (which in this env may work via some TS runner)
2. When quality-check returns with exit code 1, `execSync` THROWS
3. The catch block treats the thrown error as "no failures" — BUG

The fix would be to use `spawnSync` (which doesn't throw) or to handle the stdout from the thrown error.

Now let me also note that `node` seems to be able to run TypeScript here (since we saw the quality-check output in the error message). 

So the core issue is that when quality-check exits with a non-zero code, `execSync` throws an error, but the catch block incorrectly interprets this as a passing state rather than a quality failure. Test 3 confirms this — the quality blocking mechanism fails because the exception gets swallowed.
